channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and panic at Turkish ski resort as fire ripped through hotel where 76 died
item.description: KARTALKAYA, Turkey (AP) — As flames tore through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey, friends Esra Karakisa and Halime Cetin stood helpless as the horror unfolded before them, with people leaning out of smoke-filled rooms pleading for help and others making the harrowing
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghans fleeing Taliban urge Trump to lift refugee program suspension
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghans who fled after the Taliban seized power appealed Wednesday to U.S. President Donald Trump to exempt them from an order suspending the relocation of refugees to the United States, some saying they risked their lives to support U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fire risk and strong winds continue in Southern California with potential rain on the horizon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California was forecast to face more dangerous winds on Wednesday but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, dampening the prospects of another round of deadly wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges like tox
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Adams says NYC is coordinating with ICE as mass migrant deportations loom
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his administration has been coordinating with ICE with regard to deporting criminal illegal migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murdoch's UK tabloids apologize to Prince Harry and admit intruding on the late Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology for intruding in his life over decades and agreed to pay substantial damages to settle his privacy invasion lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US adults want border security action but mostly oppose arrests in schools, churches: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults are on board with the idea of beefing up security at the southern border and undertaking some targeted deportations, according to a poll.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing risk of sending kids to school
item.description: Fears intensified for some when the Trump administration announced Tuesday it would allow federal immigration agencies to make arrests at schools, churches and hospitals, ending a decades-old policy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look to find favorable jury as trial begins on charges he fired on ex-friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors will pack back into a Los Angeles courtroom on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the three-week trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security Tuesday, firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up and eliminated all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC subway conductor makes tragic discovery on train: dead woman
item.description: A woman between 50 and 60 years old and believed to be homeless was discovered dead on a subway train in New York City on Saturday, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont identified
item.description: A Customs and Border Protection source has identified the Border Patrol agent shot and killed during a traffic stop Monday as David Maland, 44.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, WGN 9

item.title: At least 66 dead, 51 injured in a hotel fire at a ski resort in northwestern Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire at a hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey on Tuesday killed at least 66 people, Turkey's Interior Minister said. Ali Yerlikaya said at least 51 other people were injured in the disaster. “We are in deep pain.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Bay Area tech CEOs get front-row seats at Trump inauguration
item.description: (KRON) -- The biggest tech leaders of Silicon Valley got a front-row seat at President Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday. There has been a sudden support this administration has gained from the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Sara Stinson, KRON4

item.title: Authorities say a US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont south of border
item.description: COVENTRY, Vt. (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent was fatally shot Monday on a highway in northern Vermont south of the Canadian border, authorities said. The death was confirmed by the FBI and Benjamine Huffman, acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago police reminded they can't cooperate with deportations
item.description: CHICAGO — WGN Investigates has learned Chicago Police officers are being reminded about what they "can" and "can't" do if President Donald Trump's promised immigration raids happen. The directives went into effect in 2021 but have renewed importance this week.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive orders on federal workforce, free speech
item.description: President Trump signed multiple executive orders during his inauguration parade at Capital One Arena on Monday evening, throwing pens to supporters after a signing ceremony. The signings at a small desk set up on stage in the Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Biden endures painful but active last day of presidency
item.description: When President Biden walked through the Capitol on Monday with mere minutes left in his presidency, a reporter asked how he was feeling.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WGN 9

item.title: Ratcliffe moves closer to CIA chief confirmation
item.description: John Ratcliffe took a step closer to being confirmed to lead the CIA on Monday as the Senate Intelligence Committee advanced his nomination. The panel advanced his nomination 14 to 3 shortly after President Trump was sworn into office. All three "no" votes were cast by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio State VS. Notre Dame: Facts to know before the CFP Championship kickoff
item.description: The Buckeyes and Fighting Irish meet Monday night in the College Football Playoff national championship game at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, Fox 5

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Here's who skipped Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Trump was sworn into office for a second time on Monday before an audience in the Capitol that included his predecessor, former President Biden, as well as former Presidents Obama, Clinton and George W. Bush and multiple current and former officials and lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed a threat to U.S. national security. But on the eve of his return to the White House, the president-elect is being hailed as the app's savior.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4
